title: This Beaten-Down Fintech Stock Landed a Massive $102 Million Vote of Confidence
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: North Reef Capital Management disclosed a major $102.21 million investment in Alkami Technology, acquiring 5.55 million shares in Q1 2026. Despite the stock declining 46% over the past year, the investment signals confidence in Alkami's digital banking platform and growth prospects. The company showed strong Q1 fundamentals with 29% revenue growth and improved profitability metrics.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite significant stock decline (46% YoY), the company received a major $102M institutional investment vote of confidence. Q1 2026 showed strong fundamentals: 29% YoY revenue growth to $126.1M, adjusted EBITDA improvement from $12.1M to $22.3M, 2.5M new users added, and a new $100M share buyback authorization. These operational improvements suggest the market may have overreacted to the selloff.

title: Surgery Partners Stock Down 39% as One Fund Slashes Stake by $19 Million
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Irenic Capital Management sold 1,047,583 shares of Surgery Partners (SGRY) in Q4 2025, reducing its stake to just 0.09% of assets. The stock has declined 39.7% over the past year, underperforming the S&P 500 by 51.61 percentage points. While the company's operations remain solid with 6.6% revenue growth, concerns center on high leverage with net debt to EBITDA at 4.2x-4.6x in a higher rate environment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 39.7% over the past year with significant underperformance versus S&P 500. Major fund (Irenic Capital) drastically reduced position from material holding to 0.09% of assets, signaling loss of conviction. Primary concern is elevated leverage (4.2x-4.6x net debt to EBITDA) in a higher interest rate environment, which amplifies downside risk despite solid operational metrics.
Listed as Irenic Capital's top holding at 13.7% of AUM ($99.11 million), indicating strong conviction and allocation priority by the fund manager.
Ranked as a top-4 holding in Irenic Capital's portfolio at 6.7% of AUM ($48.60 million), indicating meaningful conviction.

title: Irenic Dumps Most Papa John's Shares
author: Lawrence Rothman, Cfa
description: Irenic Capital Management LP significantly reduced its Papa John's International position by approximately 70%, selling 748,592 shares in Q4 and retaining only 325,108 shares valued at $12.5 million. The stake now represents just 0.8% of the fund's AUM, dropping out of its top five holdings. Papa John's stock has underperformed the market, declining 30.7% over the past year while the S&P 500 gained 16.4%, amid struggling North American same-store sales.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major institutional investor Irenic Capital divested ~70% of its position, reducing Papa John's from its third-largest holding to outside top five. Stock has significantly underperformed the market (-30.7% vs S&P 500 +16.4% over one year), and the company faces operational challenges with North American same-store sales declining 2.7% in Q3.
Listed as Irenic's largest holding ($99.1 million, 6.7% of AUM) but no specific news or context provided in the article regarding this position.
Listed as Irenic's second-largest holding ($67.0 million, 9.3% of AUM) but no specific news or context provided in the article regarding this position.
Listed as Irenic's third-largest holding ($51.7 million, 3.5% of AUM) but no specific news or context provided in the article regarding this position.
Listed as Irenic's fourth-largest holding ($48.6 million, 3.3% of AUM) but no specific news or context provided in the article regarding this position.

title: Alkami (ALKT) Q2 Revenue Jumps 36%
author: Jesterai
description: Alkami Technology reported strong Q2 2025 financial results, with GAAP revenue of $112.1 million, a 36.4% year-over-year increase. The company exceeded analyst estimates, demonstrated growth in registered users, and continued integrating the MANTL acquisition.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positive sentiment due to strong revenue growth (36.4% YoY), exceeding analyst estimates, increasing registered users by 2.3 million, expanding product suite to 34 products, and maintaining low client churn rate

title: Alkami Technology Beats Q4 Estimates, Strengthens Digital Banking Platform With $400 Million FinTech Acquisition
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Alkami Technology reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates, and announced a $400 million acquisition of MANTL to boost its digital banking platform. The company also provided positive guidance for FY25.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Alkami Technology reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings, provided strong FY25 guidance, and announced a strategic $400 million acquisition to strengthen its digital banking platform, indicating a positive outlook for the company.
